nodes.label: arikara | be | first
nodes.sentence: The Arikara were the first to discover maize.

nodes.label: man | go | ∅
nodes.sentence: One day, a young man went out hunting and climbed a high hill.

nodes.label: he | see | standing
nodes.sentence: Looking down into the valley, he saw a buffalo bull standing near where two rivers met.

nodes.label: he | plan | ∅
nodes.sentence: He planned to kill the buffalo but first took in the beauty of the land—the rivers’ low banks lined with many trees.

nodes.label: he | get | ∅
nodes.sentence: The buffalo faced north, so he couldn’t get close enough with his bow.

nodes.label: he | decide | ∅
nodes.sentence: He decided to wait until the buffalo moved closer to the riverbanks or to a ravine full of bushes.

nodes.label: hunter | stay | ∅
nodes.sentence: That night the hunter stayed awake, hungry and frustrated.

nodes.label: he | hurry | dawn
nodes.sentence: Before dawn, he hurried back to the hilltop.

nodes.label: buffalo | stand | ∅
nodes.sentence: The buffalo stood still, now facing east.

nodes.label: he | wait | ∅
nodes.sentence: He waited all day again, the sun going down with the buffalo still in the same spot.

nodes.label: he | eat | ∅
nodes.sentence: That night he barely ate and did not sleep well.

nodes.label: he | check | buffalo
nodes.sentence: Early the next morning, as the sun rose, he checked the buffalo.

nodes.label: it | remain | ∅
nodes.sentence: It remained standing exactly where it was, now facing south.

nodes.label: he | wait | day
nodes.sentence: He waited through the day once more, and when the sun set, the buffalo had not moved.

nodes.label: hunter | rush | morning
nodes.sentence: On the fourth morning, the hunter rushed up the hill at sunrise.

nodes.label: buffalo | stand | ∅
nodes.sentence: The buffalo still stood there, now facing west.

nodes.label: he | watch | ∅
nodes.sentence: He watched all day long, but the buffalo did not budge.

nodes.label: he | wonder | ∅
nodes.sentence: He wondered, “Why won’t the buffalo move?”

nodes.label: it | drink | ∅
nodes.sentence: It didn’t drink, eat, or sleep.

nodes.label: he | sense | ∅
nodes.sentence: He sensed some mighty power was at work.

nodes.label: he | hurry | hilltop
nodes.sentence: The following morning, he hurried to the hilltop as soon as dawn brightened the land.

nodes.label: buffalo | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: But the buffalo was gone.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | bush
nodes.sentence: In its place was a strange bush.

nodes.label: he | approach | ∅
nodes.sentence: He approached carefully and realized it was a new kind of plant.

nodes.label: he | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: Tracing the buffalo’s footprints, he saw where it had turned east, south, and west.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | track
nodes.sentence: In the center of these paths was a single track, and from that spot, the small plant had grown.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | track
nodes.sentence: There were no tracks showing the buffalo’s departure.

nodes.label: hunter | run | ∅
nodes.sentence: Excited, the hunter ran back to his village and shared his story with the chiefs and people about the mysterious buffalo and the new plant.

nodes.label: chief | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: Curious, all the chiefs and people came to see the place.

nodes.label: they | look | track
nodes.sentence: They looked closely at the buffalo tracks, noting it had stood in the same spot but had left without leaving a path behind.

nodes.label: people | understand | ∅
nodes.sentence: The people understood then that Wahkoda, the Great Spirit, had given them this strange plant.

nodes.label: they | know | plant
nodes.sentence: They already knew many plants they could eat and when each ripened.

nodes.label: they | guard | plant
nodes.sentence: So, they guarded this new plant carefully.

nodes.label: flower | blossom | it
nodes.sentence: A flower soon blossomed on it.

nodes.label: shoot | appear | one
nodes.sentence: Later, a new shoot appeared at one of its joints, covered in fine hair that started green and turned brown.

nodes.label: people | think | ∅
nodes.sentence: The people thought the fruit might be ripe but were afraid to touch it.

nodes.label: they | gather | ∅
nodes.sentence: They gathered to talk over what they should do.

nodes.label: life | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: A young man spoke up, “My life has not been good.

nodes.label: i | care | ∅
nodes.sentence: If any harm comes to me, I do not care.”

nodes.label: people | agree | ∅
nodes.sentence: Encouraged by him, the people agreed.

nodes.label: man | reach | ∅
nodes.sentence: The young man reached out, placing his hand on the plant and then on the fruit.

nodes.label: he | grasp | it
nodes.sentence: He grasped it firmly and declared, “It is solid.

nodes.label: it | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: It is ripe.”

nodes.label: he | peel | husk
nodes.sentence: He peeled back the husks and said, “It is red.”

nodes.label: he | take | some
nodes.sentence: He took some of the grains, showed them to the people, and ate several.

nodes.label: he | die | ∅
nodes.sentence: He did not die.

nodes.label: everyone | know | ∅
nodes.sentence: Now everyone knew Wahkoda had sent this plant to nourish them.

nodes.label: fall | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: When fall came and the prairie grasses turned brown, the plant’s leaves browned as well.

nodes.label: fruit | harvest | ∅
nodes.sentence: The fruit was harvested and stored.

nodes.label: ∅ | divide | ∅
nodes.sentence: After winter passed, the kernels were divided, four per family.

nodes.label: people | move | lodge
nodes.sentence: The people moved their lodges closer to where the plant had grown.

nodes.label: they | plant | seed
nodes.sentence: When the hills turned green again, they planted those seeds, first building small mounds like the one where the first plant had grown.

nodes.label: corn | grow | ∅
nodes.sentence: The corn grew tall and ripe, its ears bright with many colors—red, yellow, white, and blue.

nodes.label: plant | grow | ∅
nodes.sentence: The next year, many plants and many ears of corn grew.

nodes.label: arikara | invite | tribe
nodes.sentence: The Arikara invited other tribes to visit and shared this new food with them.

nodes.label: that | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: That is how the Omahas first came to have corn.
